title: "DtMF" Shatters All-Time Hot Latin Songs Record — 57 Consecutive Weeks at No. 1, Surpassing "Despacito"
detail: Billboard reported on April 29, 2026 (chart dated May 2) that Bad Bunny's "DtMF" (Debí Tirar Más Fotos) logged its 57th consecutive week at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot Latin Songs chart, surpassing the previous all-time record of 56 weeks held by Luis Fonsi & Daddy Yankee's "Despacito" feat. Justin Bieber (2017). "DtMF" first reached No. 1 on the chart dated January 25, 2025 — just 11 days after the album's release — and has held the top position uninterrupted ever since, spanning 16 months on top of the chart. The achievement marks the longest No. 1 run in the Billboard Hot Latin Songs chart's 60+ year history. The song is the title track from *Debí Tirar Más Fotos*, which became the first Spanish-language album to win the Grammy Award for Album of the Year (February 2026).

sources.name: Billboard — DtMF Breaks Despacito Record on Hot Latin Songs
Y94 — A New Legend at No. 1: Bad Bunny's 'DtMF' Officially Surpasses 'Despacito'

media.caption: "DtMF" breaks the Hot Latin Songs all-time record with 57 weeks at No. 1, surpassing Despacito
